root_cause.text: The archive extraction logic fails to validate whether the extracted file paths resolve to a location within the intended target directory, allowing an attacker to write files anywhere on the file system via path traversal (Zip Slip).
root_cause.evidence: The patch adds a check `if (!file.getCanonicalPath().startsWith(targetDirPath))` before extracting files in `RunJar.unJar`, and the added test case explicitly verifies that extracting a `JarEntry` with a path like `"../outside.path"` throws an `IOException` with the message "would create file outside of".

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The archive extraction logic fails to validate whether the extracted file paths resolve to a location within the intended target directory, allowing an attacker to write files anywhere on the file system via path traversal (Zip Slip).
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch adds a check `if (!file.getCanonicalPath().startsWith(targetDirPath))` before extracting files in `RunJar.unJar`, and the added test case explicitly verifies that extracting a `JarEntry` with a path like `"../outside.path"` throws an `IOException` with the message "would create file outside of".

intent_record.intent_summary: Add path boundary validation to prevent path traversal vulnerabilities during JAR extraction.
intent_record.what: Added a validation check to ensure the canonical path of the extracted file starts with the canonical path of the target directory.
intent_record.how: Prevents path traversal vulnerabilities by throwing an IOException if an archive entry attempts to extract a file outside the intended destination directory.

intent_record.intent_summary: Compute target directory canonical path to facilitate path traversal prevention.
intent_record.what: Added a line to compute the canonical path of the target directory.
intent_record.how: Provides a secure base path reference to validate extracted jar entries against path traversal.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add path traversal validation to prevent extracting files outside the target directory.
intent_record.what: Added a canonical path check against the target directory path before extracting JAR entries.
intent_record.how: Prevents path traversal vulnerabilities (like Zip Slip) by ensuring that extracted files are not created outside the intended destination directory.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add static imports for testing utilities to simplify test code.
intent_record.what: Added static imports for MATCH_ANY, fail, and any.
intent_record.how: Makes these testing utilities available without class qualification.

intent_record.intent_summary: Import StandardCharsets to provide standard character encoding definitions.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets.
intent_record.how: Makes the StandardCharsets class available to the rest of the file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a unit test to verify that unjarring prevents directory traversal vulnerabilities.
intent_record.what: Added a new unit test testUnJar2 that creates a jar file with a directory traversal entry ('../outside.path') and attempts to extract it.
intent_record.how: Validates that the RunJar.unJar method correctly prevents extracting files outside the intended destination directory by asserting that an appropriate IOException is thrown.

grouping_result.intent: Add path traversal validation to prevent extracting files outside the target directory.
Add static imports for testing utilities to simplify test code.
Import StandardCharsets to provide standard character encoding definitions.
Add a unit test to verify that unjarring prevents directory traversal vulnerabilities.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add path traversal validation to prevent extracting files outside the target directory.

relevance_review.reason: The group's core intent and hunks directly add the missing path validation required to prevent the Zip Slip vulnerability, aligning perfectly with the root cause.
The group only adds static imports for testing utilities, which is unrelated to fixing the path traversal vulnerability during archive extraction.
Importing character encoding definitions is unrelated to fixing a path traversal (Zip Slip) vulnerability during archive extraction.
The group adds a unit test specifically designed to verify that the directory traversal (Zip Slip) vulnerability is prevented, making it directly related to the vulnerability's root cause.

candidates.rationale: Creates the file object using the unvalidated entry name, which can contain path traversal characters (e.g., ../), leading to arbitrary file write.
Creates the file object using the unvalidated entry name, which can contain path traversal characters (e.g., ../), leading to arbitrary file write.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement constructs a File object using the unvalidated archive entry name. Since entry.getName() can contain directory traversal sequences (like '../'), this allows an attacker to extract files outside the intended target directory (Zip Slip vulnerability).

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: YARN-2185. Use pipes when localizing archives. Contributed by Miklos Szegedi
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 7288b08330001102f944124d18e02bf0585d3e96
Author: Jason Lowe <jlowe@apache.org>
Date:   Wed Jan 31 09:39:43 2018 -0600

    YARN-2185. Use pipes when localizing archives. Contributed by Miklos Szegedi

diff --git a/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/util/RunJar.java b/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/util/RunJar.java
index 19b51ad692a..89b7d763ec1 100644
--- a/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/ha
bic_trace_step.reason: The added `unJar` function creates the destination file path using `File file = new File(toDir, entry.getName());` without validating if the resolved path remains within `toDir`. This allows path traversal (Zip Slip) if the entry name contains '../'.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: HADOOP-13068. Clean up RunJar and related test class. (Contributed by Andras Bokor)
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit f343d91ecc0d1c6d9dc9810faf68ec04f7b07c2f
Author: Arpit Agarwal <arp@apache.org>
Date:   Wed May 4 09:49:33 2016 -0700

    HADOOP-13068. Clean up RunJar and related test class. (Contributed by Andras Bokor)

diff --git a/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/util/RunJar.java b/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/util/RunJar.java
index 52cf05c0477..19b51ad692a 100644
--- a/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/ap
bic_trace_step.reason: The `unJar` function contains the vulnerable construct: `File file = new File(toDir, entry.getName());` where the entry name from the archive is used to construct a file path without any validation to ensure it does not escape the destination directory `toDir` (Zip Slip). The commit refactors the code to use try-with-resources but does not introduce the necessary path validation.

git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/hadoop/core/branches/HADOOP-
bic_trace_step.reason: The unJar method reads entries from a JAR file and creates new File objects using 'new File(toDir, entry.getName())' on line 44 without checking if the resolved path falls within the target directory 'toDir'. This missing validation allows path traversal attacks (Zip Slip).
